revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24376438097|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:外宮参道]] {{Infobox_road |種別・系統 = {{ill|Municipal Road|ja|市町村道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal Road|1=municipal road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10276558}} |アイコン = [[File: Geku Sando, 2014 Summer.jpg|thumb]]
View from the Ise City Station side of the Outer Shrine Sandō
（{{ill|2014|en|2014|qid=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}} {{ill|August 11|en|August 11|qid=Q2778|lt=August 11|1=August 11}} photo） |名前 = Ise City Road Gekū Sandō Line{{Cite web|和書|title=第46回 Ise City Urban Planning Council Meeting Minutes – Summary|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/43305/46gijiroku.pdf|date=2017-06-01|accessdate=2017-10-03}} |名前の補足 = Outer Shrine Sandō (Nickname) |副名 = {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.24 Yamada Station Road|ja|三重県道24号山田停車場線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.24 Yamada Station Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{ill|1955|en|1955|qid=Q18577|lt=1955|1=1955}}（{{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}}30）{{ill|March 8|en|March 8|qid=Q2395|lt=March 8|1=March 8}} Mie Prefecture Notice No.179 ・ {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.24 Ise City Station Road|ja|三重県道24号伊勢市停車場線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.24 Ise City Station Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{ill|1972|en|1972|qid=Q2476|lt=1972|1=1972}}（{{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}}47）{{ill|March 31|en|March 31|qid=Q2461|lt=March 31|1=March 31}} Mie Prefecture Notice No.175-5 ・ {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.21 Ise City Station Road|ja|三重県道21号伊勢市停車場線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.21 Ise City Station Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外宮参道|1=Gekū Sando|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q42387615}}{{sfn|三重県|2016|p=39}} |副名の補足 = (Former Name) |地図画像 = |総距離 = 390.7{{ill|Metre|en|Metre|qid=Q11573|lt=m|1=Metre}} |陸上距離 = 390.7m |海上距離 = Not specified |制定年 = Prefectural Road: {{ill|1955|en|1955|qid=Q18577|lt=1955|1=1955}}（{{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}}30）
Municipal Road: {{ill|2017|en|2017|qid=Q25290|lt=2017|1=2017}}（{{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}29）{{Cite web|和書|publisher=Ise City Council|title=産業建設委員協議会記録|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/41220/290213sankenkyougikai.pdf|date=2017-02-13|accessdate=2017-10-03}} |開通年 = {{ill|1899|en|1899|qid=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}（{{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}32）{{sfn|宇治山田市役所 編|1929|p=741}} |廃止年 = Prefectural Road: 2017（{{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}29）{{sfn|三重県|2016|p=39}} |道の方位 = |起点 = {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Ise City Stop|1=Iseshi Station}}{{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}}（{{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}19）{{ill|March 30|en|March 30|qid=Q2460|lt=March 30|1=March 30}} Mie Prefecture Notice No.267 |道路の方角 = |環状線通過都市 = |主な経由国 = |主な経由都市 = |終点 = Mie Prefecture {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise City|1=Ise, Mie}} |終点方角 = |接続する主な道路 = {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.22 Ise South Island Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.22 Ise South Island Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357908}}
{{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.32 Ise Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357920}}
{{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.37 Toba Matsusaka Line|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.37 Toba Matsusaka Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357923}} }} [[File:Mie-ken_Line_21_North_side.jpg|thumb|Same location as above (photo taken on {{ill|October 3|en|October 3|qid=Q2931|lt=October 3|1=October 3}}, {{ill|2008|en|2008|qid=Q2004|lt=2008|1=2008}})]] '''Gekū Sandō''' ({{ill|Geku Sandō|ja|げくうさんどう|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geku Sandō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a {{ill|Road|en|Road|qid=Q34442|lt=road|1=Road}} passing through {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise City|1=Ise, Mie}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, connecting {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Ise City Station|1=Iseshi Station}} and {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} / {{ill|Toyouke Grand Shrine|ja|豊受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Toyōke Daijingū|qid=Q11633343}} (Outer Shrine){{Cite web|和書|publisher={{ill|Chunichi Shimbun|en|Chunichi Shimbun|qid=Q904678|lt=Chūnichi Shimbun|1=Chunichi Shimbun}}|title=Yudai Nakahira "Restoration of the Outer Shrine 'Sandō' Revives Bustle – Member Stores at 1.5 Times Their Level 10 Years Ago, with Steps Removed and Stone Paving Installed; Nickname Also Changed"|url=http://example.com|date={{ill|2013|en|2013|qid=Q1998|lt=2013|1=2013}}-{{ill|October 5|en|October 5|qid=Q2930|lt=October 5|1=October 5}}|accessdate=2017th year 10月10日}}. Along the route, a {{ill|Shōtengai|en|Shōtengai|qid=Q96288394|lt=shopping street|1=Shōtengai}} (Outer Shrine Sandō Development Association) has developed. Until its transfer to Ise City in {{ill|March 2017|ja|2017th year 3月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 2017|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was a major regional road and was known as {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.21 Ise City Station Road|ja|三重県道21号伊勢市停車場線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.21 Ise City Station Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外宮参道|1=Gekū Sando|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q42387615}} (みえけんどう21ごう いせしていしゃじょうせん){{sfn|三重県|2016|p=39}}. After the transfer to Ise City, its official name became '''Ise City Road Gekū Sandō Line''' ({{ill|Ise City Road Gekū Sandō Line|ja|いせしどうげくうさんどうせん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Road Gekū Sandō Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) == Overview == === Route Data During the Prefectural Road Era === As of {{ill|April 1, 2016|ja|2016th year 4月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 1, 2016|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}28){{sfn|三重県|2016|p=39}}. * Starting point: Ise City Stop{{sfn|三重県|2016|p=39}}{{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Ise City Stop|1=Iseshi Station}} (located at Honmachi, Ise City, at address Katacho 186-3, near the Ise City Station south exit at the station intersection) * Ending point: Ise City, Mie Prefecture{{sfn|三重県|2016|p=39}} (located at Honmachi, Ise City, at address 121-11, in front of the Outer Shrine intersection) * Total length: 390.7m{{sfn|三重県|2016|p=39}} ** Actual length: 390.7m{{sfn|三重県|2016|p=39}} == Route Conditions == === Usage Conditions === ; Automobile Traffic Volume{{Cite web|和書|publisher={{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|en|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|qid=Q1376196|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|1=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism}}|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/road/census/h22-1/data/pdf/kasyo24.pdf|title=FY2010 Road Traffic Census – Basic Table by Location for General Traffic|accessdate=2017th year 10月10日}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/road/census/h27/data/pdf/kasyo24.pdf|title=FY2015 National Road and Street Traffic Conditions Survey – Basic Table by Location for General Traffic|work=FY2015 National Road and Street Traffic Conditions Survey|publisher={{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Road Bureau|ja|国土交通省道路局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Road Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2017th year 10月10日}} {| class="wikitable" style="background-color:#fee0fb; width:40%" |- |style="color:#fff; background-color:#33ff00; text-align:center" rowspan="2"|'''Location''' |style="color:#fff; background-color:#33ff00; text-align:center"|'''12-Hour Weekday''' |style="color:#fff; background-color:#33ff00; text-align:center"|'''24-Hour Weekday''' |- |style="color:#fff; background-color:#33ff00" |'''FY2005'''⇒'''FY2015''' |style="color:#fff; background-color:#33ff00" |'''FY2005'''⇒'''FY2015''' |- | Honmachi, Ise City |style="text-align:right"|878 vehicles⇒ 483 vehicles |style="text-align:right"|1,071 vehicles⇒ 580 vehicles |} == Geography == === Municipalities Passed === * {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} ** {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise City|1=Ise, Mie}} === Major Connecting Roads === * {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.201 Ujiyamada Port Ise City Station Road|ja|三重県道201号宇治山田港伊勢市停車場線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.201 Ujiyamada Port Ise City Station Road|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 201|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357902}}: Starting point (Ise City Station intersection) * {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.22 Ise South Island Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.22 Ise South Island Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357908}} ・ {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.37 Toba Matsusaka Line|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.37 Toba Matsusaka Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357923}} ({{ill|Miyuki Road|ja|御幸道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki Road|1=Miyuki Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22222736}}, overlapping section): Honmachi, Ise City * {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.32 Ise Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.32 Ise Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357920}}: Ending point (Outer Shrine intersection) === Surroundings === [[File: Ise Shrine Geku (New shrine).jpg|thumb]] Along the route, numerous restaurants and souvenir shops catering to pilgrims line the street{{sfn|JTBパブリッシング西日本支社 編|2017|p=52}}. Some establishments even offer paid luggage storage services{{sfn|JTBパブリッシング西日本支社 編|2017|p=52}}. In the northern part of the sandō (in front of Ise City Station), several inns, a souvenir shop operated by Ise Shinsen, Japanese cuisine restaurants, and cafés have opened{{sfn|JTBパブリッシング西日本支社 編|2017|pp=54-57}}. * {{ill|JR Ise Sangu Line|ja|JR参宮線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Ise Sangu Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135504266}} ・ {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|qid=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}}) {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Ise City Station|1=Iseshi Station}} * Ise Shinsen * {{ill|Meiji Yasuda Life|en|Meiji Yasuda Life|qid=Q174081|lt=Meiji Yasuda Life Insurance|1=Meiji Yasuda Life}} Ise Office * Ise Kikuichi * Mie {{ill|Kintetsu Taxi|ja|近鉄タクシー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Taxi|1=Kintetsu Taxi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11639069}} Ise Office * Yamada Hall * {{ill|Yamamura Milk|ja|山村乳業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Milk|1=Yamamura Milk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17230620}} Yamamura Milk School * Ise Sekiya Main Store * {{ill|Buta Sute|ja|豚捨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buta Sute|1=Butasute|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65283157}} Outer Shrine Front Store * Outer Shrine Post Office (Ise) * {{ill|Akafuku|ja|赤福|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akafuku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 赤福餅|1=Akafuku Mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q736196}} Outer Shrine Special Store * Ise City Tourism Association Headquarters * {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} / {{ill|Toyouke Grand Shrine|ja|豊受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Toyōke Daijingū|qid=Q11633343}} (Outer Shrine) == History == In {{ill|1899|en|1899|qid=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}32), a new road was opened from Yamada Station (present-day Ise City Station) to the Outer Shrine{{sfn|宇治山田市役所 編|1929|p=741}}. Along the route, inns were built in succession and the area was once regarded as one of Japan’s three major inn districts{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.jalan.net/news/article/118746/|title=Newly Opened – Enjoy a Leisurely Stroll along the Ise Grand Shrine Outer Shrine Sandō [Mie]|publisher={{ill|Recruit (company)|en|Recruit (company)|qid=Q2135836|lt=Recruit Holdings|1=Recruit (company)}}|author=じゃらん編集部|work=じゃらんニュース|date={{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}}-{{ill|October 23|en|October 23|qid=Q2958|lt=October 23|1=October 23}}|accessdate=2017th year 10月10日}}. However, the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} in {{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}}20) destroyed many inns, delaying reconstruction and causing the area to lose its character as an accommodation district{{sfn|日本地誌研究所 編|1976|p=353}}. On {{ill|March 8|en|March 8|qid=Q2395|lt=March 8, 1955|1=March 8}}, it was designated as {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.24 Yamada Station Road|ja|三重県道24号山田停車場線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.24 Yamada Station Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}}30) with its starting point set at “Yamada Station” and its ending point at “Toyokawa Town, Ujiyamada City”. On {{ill|March 31|en|March 31|qid=Q2461|lt=March 31, 1972|1=March 31}}, it was renamed to {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.24 Ise City Station Road|ja|三重県道24号伊勢市停車場線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.24 Ise City Station Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At that time, the starting and ending points were not changed. In {{ill|1983|en|1983|qid=Q2434|lt=1983|1=1983}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}}58), with the undergrounding of power lines and the installation of a tree planting strip, the road was nicknamed “{{ill|Living street|en|Living street|qid=Q1628979|lt=Community Road|1=Living street}} Shingu Sandō”, and in {{ill|2006|en|2006|qid=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}18) stone pavement was installed, changing the nickname to “Gekū Sandō”. On {{ill|March 30|en|March 30|qid=Q2460|lt=March 30, 2007|1=March 30}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}19), it was renamed to {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.21 Ise City Station Road|ja|三重県道21号伊勢市停車場線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.21 Ise City Station Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外宮参道|1=Gekū Sando|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q42387615}}, with the starting point changed to “Ise City Station” and the ending point to “Ise City”. On {{ill|February 13|en|February 13|qid=Q2333|lt=February 13, 2017|1=February 13}}, at a meeting of the Ise City Industrial Construction Committee, it was announced that by having Ise City assume control over both the Outer Shrine Sandō and the Outer Shrine Front Plaza, event organization and traffic regulation would be facilitated, and the road would be transferred from Mie Prefecture to Ise City. Then, on {{ill|March 31|en|March 31|qid=Q2461|lt=March 31, 2017|1=March 31}}, the {{ill|Mie Prefectural Road No.21 Ise City Station Road|ja|三重県道21号伊勢市停車場線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Road No.21 Ise City Station Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外宮参道|1=Gekū Sando|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q42387615}} was abolished and transferred to Ise City{{ill|2017|en|2017|qid=Q25290|lt=2017|1=2017}}（{{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}29）{{ill|March 31|en|March 31|qid=Q2461|lt=March 31|1=March 31}} Mie Prefecture Notice No.247. After its transition to a municipal road, its name became “Ise City Road Gekū Sandō Line”. == From Shingu Sandō to Gekū Sandō == Following the {{ill|Sengu Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengu Shikinen Sengu|qq=}} in {{ill|1993|en|1993|qid=Q2065|lt=1993|1=1993}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}5), the {{ill|World Exposition|ja|世界祝祭博覧会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World Exposition|1=World Festival Exposition|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11362420}} in {{ill|1994|en|1994|qid=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}6), and the opening of {{ill|Shima Spain Village|en|Shima Spain Village|qid=Q16669013|lt=Shima Spain Village|1=Shima Spain Village}} in {{ill|1995|en|1995|qid=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}7) and thereafter, the number of visitors to the Outer Shrine decreased while tourists increasingly concentrated in front of the Inner Shrine, leading to a decline in the Outer Shrine area{{Cite web|和書|publisher={{ill|Chunichi Shimbun|en|Chunichi Shimbun|qid=Q904678|lt=Chūnichi Shimbun|1=Chunichi Shimbun}}|title=Yudai Nakahira "New Developments for Sustained Vitality – One Year of Ise Grand Shrine’s ‘Okage-doshi’ Recalled"|url=http://example.com|date={{ill|2014|en|2014|qid=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}}-{{ill|December 30|en|December 30|qid=Q2901|lt=December 30|1=December 30}}|accessdate=unknown}}. Recognizing that shop owners operating in front of the Outer Shrine lacked sufficient knowledge of it, study sessions on the Outer Shrine were initiated by shop owners from {{ill|2003|en|2003|qid=Q1986|lt=2003|1=2003}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}15) to establish an {{ill|Omotenashi|en|Omotenashi|qid=Q18383366|lt=omotenashi|1=Omotenashi}} system{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chusho.meti.go.jp/keiei/sapoin/monozukuri300sha/shoutengai30/2014/S14.pdf|title=Visualizing the Ideal by Reassessing Regional Resources – Aiming for a Community-Integrated Shopping Street|publisher={{ill|Small and Medium Enterprise Agency|ja|中小企業庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Small and Medium Enterprise Agency|1=Small and Medium Enterprise Agency|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11363903}}|date=2014|accessdate=2017th year 10月10日}}. In {{ill|2006|en|2006|qid=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}18) the nickname “Shingu Sandō” was changed to “Gekū Sandō”. From {{ill|2009|en|2009|qid=Q1996|lt=2009|1=2009}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}21) the Ise Chamber of Commerce and Industry led initiatives to promote visits to the Outer Shrine. In {{ill|2011|en|2011|qid=Q1994|lt=2011|1=2011}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}23), a cutlery store, Kikuichimon Hon-ten, which had to close due to a lack of successors, was revived as a community hub under the name “Ise Kikuichi”, and in {{ill|2012|en|2012|qid=Q1990|lt=2012|1=2012}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}24), with the opening of Sengūkan aimed at revitalizing the area in front of the Outer Shrine, the Outer Shrine Sandō Development Association initiated activities to transform the shopping street into a sandō. This sparked a growing local awareness of revitalizing the area in front of the Outer Shrine, and along Gekū Sandō, lodging facilities and restaurants proliferated; although about 7 stores closed in the three years up to {{ill|2014|en|2014|qid=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}26), nearly 30 new stores opened. Foot traffic, especially among women and young people, surged so that even the year following the 1993 renewal ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}25, i.e. {{ill|2013|en|2013|qid=Q1998|lt=2013|1=2013}}) continued to be lively. == Footnote == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=宇治山田市役所|title=宇治山田市史 上巻|publisher=宇治山田市役所|date=1929th year 1月20日|page=862|ref={{sfnref|宇治山田市役所 編|1929}}}}{{全国書誌番号|54006369}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=JTBパブリッシング西日本支社|title={{ill|Rurubu|ja|るるぶ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rurubu|1=RURUBU|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11281544}} Ise-Shima '18|series=Rurubu Information Magazine Kinki② Issue 5133|publisher={{ill|JTB Publishing|ja|JTBパブリッシング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JTB Publishing|1=JTB
